name: Scaleway Object Storage
description: Scaleway Object Storage — S3-compatible object storage from a French/EU sovereign cloud. ~€7/TB/month storage; 75GB/month egress free, then $10/TB. Bucket-scoped REST with AWS SigV4 against s3.{region}.scw.cloud. Regions: fr-par, nl-ams, pl-waw. Generate API keys at console.scaleway.com → IAM → API Keys; the access key + secret pair maps to access_key_id + secret_access_key here.

credential_fields.name: region
credential_fields.label: Region
credential_fields.description: Scaleway region of your bucket — fr-par (Paris), nl-ams (Amsterdam), or pl-waw (Warsaw).

credential_fields.name: access_key_id
credential_fields.label: Scaleway Access Key
credential_fields.description: From console.scaleway.com → IAM → API Keys → Generate API key. Scope to an application or IAM group with object-storage permissions.

credential_fields.name: secret_access_key
credential_fields.label: Scaleway Secret Key
credential_fields.description: Returned once at creation alongside the access key. Regenerate if lost.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.description: List objects in a bucket (S3-compatible ListObjectsV2). Returns XML; parse Contents[].Key / Size / LastModified, plus IsTruncated + NextContinuationToken for pagination.

prefix.description: Filter to keys starting with this prefix.

continuation-token.description: Pagination cursor from a prior IsTruncated response.

tools.name: put_object
tools.description: Upload an object. Pass content as 'body'; runner sends verbatim with SigV4 integrity hash. Scaleway caps single PUT at 5GB; use multipart for larger.

body.description: Object content. Bytes welcome — the runner sends them verbatim.

tools.name: get_object
tools.description: Download an object. Response body is the raw object bytes (response cap 200MB).

tools.name: delete_object
tools.description: Delete a single object. Returns 204 on success.

tools.name: list_buckets
tools.description: List all Scaleway buckets the access key can see. Returns XML; ListAllMyBucketsResult.Buckets.Bucket[] has names + creation dates.
